title: bud-...-base-as-default : fix dup-arm flake
body:
Some sort of weird error with duplicate "arm" string in
manifest output. Started blowing up in podman CI today.

Fixes: #4520

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
They're not flakes. Buildah is actually broken: #4503
No, the two "arm" values are pulled out of "linux/arm/v6" and "linux/arm/v7" stanzas, respectively.  The test is right.
Thanks. Closing, and leaving this in the hands of someone else.
